method_atom: A scheme that encodes information non-locally in the degenerate collective states of a many-body system whose localized excitations obey non-commuting (non-Abelian) exchange statistics, so that adiabatically exchanging (braiding) the excitations enacts unitary transformations determined only by the topology of their exchange paths rather than by local details. Because the encoding is global rather than local, the stored information is intrinsically protected against local perturbations, and the transformations form a representation of the braid group acting on a topologically degenerate state space.
object_atom: Quasiparticle excitations of topological states of matter used to build a fault-tolerant quantum computer, chiefly the non-Abelian fractional quantum Hall states (archetypally the nu=5/2 state), with further candidate hosts in ultra-cold atoms in optical lattices and thin-film superconductors.
brief_reason: The non-local topological encoding and braid-group/non-Abelian-statistics machinery is the transferable method, while the anyonic quasiparticles of fractional-quantum-Hall and related condensed-matter systems are the physics-specific object.
